page: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DLSv5 family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX-512 Foundation and Vector Neural Network Instructions) and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA extensions.

This instance does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring reliance on remote premium storage options for persistent disk operations. It is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or hardware GPU accelerators. The primary tradeoff of the D48ls_v5 is its low memory density relative to its high compute capacity, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive workloads but enhances its cost efficiency for pure CPU-bound tasks.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-threaded performance, particularly in bzip3 decompression where it achieved 221.23 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. It also demonstrates strong performance in PassMark memory tests with a score of 2837.7 and PassMark CPU encryption tests with a score of 43318.73. In LLM inference benchmarks using small (135M) and medium (7B) models, the instance achieved strong results, processing 199.49 tokens/sec for medium model prompts. However, performance scales down to average levels for larger workloads, such as 70B model inference and standard single-core operations.

The D48ls_v5 is suited for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local scratch storage. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, image processing, cryptographic operations, medium-scale language model inference, and batch processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz, it delivers 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on remote premium storage. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-threaded capabilities, with bzip3 decompression reaching 221.23 GB/sec and strong performance in CPU encryption and medium-model LLM inference. Conversely, single-core performance and large-model inference remain in the average tier. This instance is designed for CPU-intensive, low-memory workloads such as software compilation, image processing, cryptography, and medium-scale batch processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-threaded and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is a compute-optimized instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and strong multi-threaded performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated Intel vCPUs and a low memory-to-core ratio for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a low 2.0 GB per core ratio
Strong multi-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression and cryptography
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring remote premium storage
Best suited for software compilation, image processing, and medium LLM inference
